ResourceHeader.ResourceName: Measuring Directivity to Determine Electron Anisotropy (MeDDEA)
ResourceHeader.Description: MeDDEA, Measuring Directivity to Determine Electron Anisotropy, is the hard X-ray directivity instrument on the PADRE, Solar Polarization and Directivity X-Ray Experiment, 12U solar-spinning CubeSat mission. MeDDEA uses flight-spare detectors from the Spectrometer/Telescope for Imaging X-rays, STIX, provided by CEA-Saclay. Unlike STIX, MeDDEA does not use imaging grids or focus on hard X-ray imaging. Instead, it is designed to make precision measurements of solar flare X-ray directivity. MeDDEA observations are used to investigate the directivity of solar flare X-ray emissions and to advance understanding of solar flare electron anisotropy, particle acceleration, and energy release in the solar corona.
